title: Generalized Kepler Problems
speaker: Guowu Meng
affiliation: Hong Kong University of Science & Technology; Joint Member, School of Mathematics & Natural Sciences

proof_stages.label: Ordinary Kepler problem as superintegrable prototype
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the classical and quantum Kepler problem as the prototype: inverse-square dynamics yield conic orbits through angular momentum and the Lenz vector, hidden SO(4) superintegrability, and quantum bound states organized by SO(4) and SO(2,4).

proof_stages.label: Early high-dimensional and magnetized generalizations
proof_stages.summary: Known generalizations replace R^3 by higher-dimensional punctured space and add monopole magnetic charge, motivating a broader search beyond isolated Dirac and Yang cases.

proof_stages.label: Light-cone reformulation and conformal symmetry
proof_stages.summary: Recasting conics as plane sections of the future light cone turns the Kepler problem into dynamics on the punctured light cone in Minkowski space and explains the appearance of the conformal group SO(2,4).

proof_stages.label: Euclidean Jordan algebras and TKK conformal algebra
proof_stages.summary: Euclidean Jordan algebras are defined and classified, and the Tits-Kantor-Koecher construction builds from each simple algebra a conformal Lie algebra with vector, structure-algebra, and covector pieces.

proof_stages.label: Universal Hamiltonian and Lenz algebra
proof_stages.summary: Using the identity element in the Jordan algebra, universal formulas for the Hamiltonian and Lenz vector are written inside the conformal algebra and shown to satisfy rotation, conservation, and Lenz commutation relations.

proof_stages.label: Quantization on rank cones and scalar-type spectra
proof_stages.summary: The conformal algebra is realized by anti-Hermitian differential operators on L2-spaces over the rank-k cones C_k with Kepler metric and measure, producing scalar-type generalized Kepler Hamiltonians with Wallach-parameter bound-state spectra.

proof_stages.label: Magnetic charge and vector-type completion
proof_stages.summary: Adding magnetic charge, interpreted as choosing suitable compact gauge-group representations and satisfying anomaly cancellation, extends the scalar construction to vector-type models and yields one generalized Kepler problem for each nontrivial unitary lowest-weight representation.

proof_stages.label: Representation-theoretic and physical outlook
proof_stages.summary: The construction is presented as enriching symmetric-domain representation theory, relating to Howe/Langlands-style correspondences, illustrating duality and anomaly ideas, and opening links with algebraic geometry, number theory, and speculative physics.
